stocks.investment_thesis: Vor Capital, a London-based long/short hedge fund with a prior history as a significant Schibsted/Vend shareholder, has flagged below the 5% disclosure threshold — the classic 'seller identified' signal. This comes against a backdrop of a stock trading 19% below the 12-month consensus target of NOK 325 and a NOK 2bn share buyback programme that has deployed ~NOK 1.5bn to date. The regulatory overhang from the NCA dawn raid on Nettbil (suspected illegal cooperation) remains unresolved, and Vor's exit — by a fund specialising in 'defensible niche monopolies' — may signal either position trimming due to risk or profit-taking from a higher-cost basis.

key_claims.claim: Vor Capital has flagged below 5% ownership in VEND (the 'seller in VEND')
key_claims.verified_via: NewsWeb message 664544 cited in email; AIM Group confirmed Vor Capital held 5.05% of Schibsted/Vend (as of Aug 2024); the flagging-under-5% event on 30 Jan 2026 is consistent with known ownership trajectory
key_claims.note: Vor Capital is a verified London-based hedge fund (FCA-regulated, founded 2017 by Brant Rubin) specialising in concentrated long/short equity in consumer, media and internet — Vend fits its investment mandate precisely. Flagging below 5% is a standard Norwegian Securities Trading Act disclosure event and is credible.

key_claims.claim: NOK 2bn share buyback programme ongoing, due to complete by 23 June 2026
key_claims.verified_via: Multiple TipRanks corporate announcement filings and vend.com IR; as of early March 2026, ~NOK 1.5bn repurchased
key_claims.note: Programme announced 12 November 2025; ~NOK 1.5bn executed by early March 2026 across Oslo Børs, CBOE, Aquis, Turquoise. Buyback is active and well-documented.

key_claims.claim: NCA conducted dawn raid at Nettbil premises (suspected illegal cooperation), not at FINN
key_claims.verified_via: Vend company statement 29 Jan 2026 on TradingView/MarketScreener; Finansavisen 29 Jan 2026; AIM Group 1 Feb 2026
key_claims.note: Multiple independent sources confirm the unannounced NCA inspection at Nettbil on 29 Jan 2026. Vend explicitly confirmed FINN was not inspected. NCA's stated purpose was to confirm or dismiss suspicion of illegal cooperation. Investigation outcome remains open.

key_claims.claim: VEND has generated ~NOK 4.5bn in asset sale proceeds in Q1 2026 (Mittanbud + Lendo + Adevinta Spain)
key_claims.verified_via: Vend.com IR press release 10 March 2026; MarketScreener 10 March 2026; TipRanks 10 March 2026
key_claims.note: Lendo Group sale to Clar Global AB completed 10 March 2026; combined with Mittanbud and Adevinta Spain distribution (~EUR 282m / ~NOK 3.2bn), total Q1 2026 proceeds verified at ~NOK 4.5bn. Company confirmed intent to return to shareholders.

key_claims.claim: Analyst consensus target NOK 325.25 vs current price ~NOK 262-264, with HOLD consensus
key_claims.verified_via: Financial data provided; TipRanks noted most recent individual analyst Buy at NOK 375 target; 12-analyst consensus framework consistent with observed HOLD stance
key_claims.note: Wide target range (NOK 258-417) reflects genuine disagreement on NCA/regulatory risk vs. capital return upside. HOLD consensus is reasonable given binary NCA risk and significant capital return catalyst.

stocks.bull_case: NOK 4.5bn in Q1 2026 asset sale proceeds to be distributed to shareholders on top of the ongoing NOK 2bn buyback (NOK 1.5bn deployed) creates a powerful capital return story. At NOK 262, stock trades at a 19% discount to NOK 325 consensus target. Pure-play Nordic marketplace focus after Lendo, Mittanbud, Prisjakt divestments sharpens earnings quality. EBITDA margins >20% with Mobility, Jobs, Real Estate verticals all >35% margin. Vor Capital's exit removes an overhang and opens space for replacement long buyers.
stocks.bear_case: NCA investigation at Nettbil for suspected illegal cooperation is a binary regulatory risk — penalties and remedies remain undefined and could be material. Vor Capital's exit below 5% by a specialist marketplace fund (after previously building to 5.05%) is a negative signal from a smart, sector-knowledgeable holder. Stock is -6.6% YTD and has underperformed since the buyback announcement. Broker (Løkbakken) has no formal coverage of VEND; outside core energy/shipping universe. HOLD consensus limits re-rating momentum.
broker_flags: VEND is OUTSIDE Løkbakken's core energy/shipping/offshore coverage universe — analysis should be treated as monitoring note only, not a primary investment recommendation
Broker track record on VEND shows inconsistent scoring (78/10 and 3/10 on prior emails) — reliability is uncertain
Email contains no formal price target, no explicit BUY/SELL recommendation — purely a regulatory/ownership news flag
Vor Capital flagging below 5% is the sole investment signal in this email; no fundamental update included
stocks.forum_sentiment: Norwegian professional investor community (e.g., Næringsforeningen 'Aksjeåret 2026' panel) named VEND the most-mentioned stock pick for 2026 at the start of the year, suggesting broadly positive domestic institutional sentiment, though the NCA raid and YTD underperformance will have tempered near-term enthusiasm.
stocks.insider_read: Vend's Employee Share Saving Plan (ESSP) was explicitly continued into 2026, allowing primary insiders to buy shares — a mild positive insider alignment signal, though no large open-market insider purchases have been flagged.
stocks.short_interest_read: No specific short interest data available from search results; however, Vor Capital's reduction below 5% removes a significant institutional long, which could temporarily increase borrow availability and modestly ease short-selling pressure or attract new short-sellers on NCA risk.
stocks.catalyst_note: Three near-term catalysts: (1) Update on structure and timing of NOK 4.5bn shareholder capital distribution (management guided update 'in due course' per 10 March 2026 press release); (2) Next earnings on 30 April 2026 — first pure-play marketplace results post-Lendo/Mittanbud divestment; (3) NCA investigation resolution at Nettbil — timeline unknown but any clearance would be a strong re-rating trigger. Vor Capital's exit below 5% is itself a minor negative near-term catalyst.
stocks.vs_consensus: At NOK 262, stock trades ~19% below 12-analyst consensus target of NOK 325 (range NOK 258-417). The current price is near the bottom of the analyst target range (NOK 258 floor), suggesting the market is pricing in meaningful NCA and execution risk. A full capital return announcement could close much of the gap to consensus.
stocks.one_line_verdict: Vor Capital's exit below 5% removes a key marketplace-specialist shareholder and is a mild negative signal, but the NOK 4.5bn capital return catalyst and 19% discount to consensus target keep VEND on the monitor list — NCA investigation outcome at Nettbil remains the binary gating factor before upgrading to actionable.

thread_narrative: This is a single-line regulatory/ownership alert from Løkbakken flagging that Vor Capital — a London hedge fund that previously built a known 5%+ stake in Schibsted/Vend — has now crossed back below the 5% disclosure threshold, identifying them as the seller. The email is terse and contains no fundamental analysis or formal recommendation. The timing (30 January 2026) is notable: it falls one day after the NCA dawn raid on Nettbil (29 January 2026), which the prior thread already flagged as a binary regulatory risk. Vor Capital's reduction is consistent with a risk-off response to that raid by a specialist fund with deep Nordic marketplace expertise. However, context from subsequent searches reveals a powerful counterweight: Vend has since completed NOK 4.5bn in Q1 2026 asset sales (Lendo, Mittanbud, Adevinta Spain) and intends to return all proceeds to shareholders, on top of a NOK 2bn buyback already ~75% executed. The stock's YTD underperformance (-6.6%) and proximity to the analyst target floor (NOK 258) suggest the market has already partially priced in the NCA risk. The thread's analytical value is low as a standalone alert but materially higher when read in conjunction with the prior NCA and buyback entries.
overall_email_quality_note: Single-line ownership alert with no fundamental analysis, no price target, no formal recommendation, and no contextualisation of the Vor Capital exit in light of the concurrent NCA investigation. Useful as a raw news flag for followers of the stock, but the broker's outside-coverage-universe limitation and the absence of any analytical framework make this a weak research communication. The email's value derives entirely from the underlying NewsWeb disclosure, not from Løkbakken's own analysis.
_email_subject: ** VEND -> det var selgeren i VEND. Vor Capital flagger under 5%.
